question: Explain the material of the steam generator tubes and their heat exchange performance.

nuscale_ch05_0022.title: Decay Heat Removal System
nuscale_ch05_0022.summary: The Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) is an engineered safety feature designed to remove residual and core decay heat from the reactor after shutdown, ensuring safe cooling without external power during non-LOCA events. It maintains the reactor coolant system (RCS) temperature below 420°F within 36 hours, preventing fuel or pressure boundary limits from being exceeded, and its reliability and risk significance are evaluated through established programs. The DHRS operates independently of the Emergency Core Cooling System (ECCS), but both can function together if needed.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the NuScale reactor pressurizer, specifying the alloys and standards for each component, such as the baffle plate, heater bundles, cover plate, and bolting. It emphasizes the use of corrosion-resistant materials compliant with ASME BPVC Section II specifications to ensure compatibility and durability in the reactor coolant system.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: The steam generators (SGs) in each NuScale Power Module are integrated within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which also houses other key components such as the pressurizer and safety valves. The SG system includes feedwater and main steam piping, plenum access ports, and relief valves, with the RPV itself forming the outer pressure boundary for the SGs. The design ensures separation between the primary and secondary sides, with specific components forming the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch05_0002.title: Summary Description
nuscale_ch05_0002.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, relying on integrated components within each NuScale Power Module (NPM). The RCS is designed to safely transfer heat from the reactor core, maintain proper coolant chemistry and boron concentration, and ensure fuel design limits are not exceeded during all operating conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain fuel integrity, minimize solid deposits, and ensure uniform boron concentration under all operating conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for reactor components.

edges.description: Section B identifies the steam generators as components within the reactor coolant system, which Section A describes as being cooled by the decay heat removal system, thus linking the steam generators' material and heat exchange performance to the DHRS's ability to remove decay heat.

Section A describes the function and design basis of the Decay Heat Removal System, which relies on the Reactor Coolant System detailed in Section B for primary coolant circulation, establishing that the DHRS's performance depends on the RCS's configuration and components such as the steam generator tubes.

Section A provides detailed material specifications for pressurizer components, while Section B gives a general overview of the reactor coolant system, including the pressurizer as one of its subsystems.

Section A provides detailed information about the components within the reactor pressure vessel, including the steam generators, while Section B gives a general overview of the reactor coolant system and its subsystems, establishing the context in which the steam generators operate.

Section A describes the overall reactor coolant system, which includes the steam generator tubes as part of its components, while Section B details the decay heat removal system that relies on the heat exchange performance of these tubes to remove residual heat during specific events.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system, while Section B references specific sections for material details of pressurizer components, indicating that Section B specifies where to find detailed material information for components broadly described in Section A.
Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system, while Section B specifies the components within the system, including the steam generators relevant to the analysis question.

Section A lists references that include material reliability and chemistry guidelines relevant to the steam generator tubes, while Section B describes the Decay Heat Removal System's reliance on the heat exchange performance of these tubes to achieve safe shutdown, connecting material properties to system performance.
Section B references Section 5.2, which is detailed in Section A, to specify the material of the RPV upper shell and upper head, thereby connecting the material specifications across both sections.
Section A provides references to material standards and reliability guidelines that are relevant to the material selection and performance of the steam generator tubes described in Section B.

Section A provides a general description of the reactor coolant system design, while Section B lists references that include material reliability and chemistry guidelines relevant to the selection and performance of steam generator tube materials.
